title: Section 232 Tariffs Extended to Hundreds of Steel and Aluminum Derivative Products
bodyHtml:
The U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) has announced that a 50 percent Section 232 tariff will be applied to additional steel and aluminum derivative products classified under 407 HTSUS numbers.

For steel derivatives, the newly included subheadings cover products under Chapters 4, 21, 27, 28, 29, 30, 32, 33, 34, 35, 38, 39, 72, 73, 76, 82, 83, 84, 85, 86, 87, and 94.

For aluminum derivatives, the additions cover products under Chapters 4, 21, 27, 29, 30, 32, 33, 34, 35, 37, 38, 73, 76, 83, 84, 85, 87, and 94.

This change took effect as of 12:01 a.m. (EDT) on August 18 with respect to covered products entered for consumption or withdrawn from warehouse. There appears to be no exception for goods already shipped to the U.S. as of that date.

A list of affected HTSUS numbers is included in BIS’s official notice. BIS notes that Section 232 tariffs will apply to the steel and aluminum content of these products, while the non-steel and non-aluminum content will remain subject to “reciprocal” and other applicable tariffs.

This change follows the first implementation of BIS’s new process, under which requests to extend Section 232 tariffs to additional products may be submitted during two-week windows three times a year (at the beginning of May, September, and January).

All products requested for inclusion during the first application period have been added to the derivative product list, with the exception of 60 HTSUS numbers that remain under ongoing investigations pursuant to Section 232 or other trade statutes. This indicates that a similarly broad approach is likely to be followed in future application periods.

 Click here for the full list of steel and steel derivative products.

Click here for the full list of aluminum and aluminum derivative products.
